_disclaimer: SYNTHETIC demo data for DueProcess. The summons, parties, case number, address, and dates are invented for demonstration only. This is NOT a real court filing and NOT legal advice.

notice.description: A realistic but synthetic California SUM-130 Summons (Unlawful Detainer) with an attached proof of service showing personal service.
notice.note: extraction.ts reads these from the image; values are perception output and may vary slightly. serviceDateISO + serviceMethod feed the deadline engine.

expected.rationale: 10 court days after personal service, counting from the day after service, skipping weekends and Juneteenth (observed 2026-06-19). Per CCP § 1167 as amended by AB 2347 (eff. 2025-01-01). The pre-2025 '5 days' rule is OUTDATED.

inCorpus.questionText: How many days do I have to file my Answer after being served the Summons and Complaint?
inCorpus.expectedBehavior: Grounded answer citing California Courts self-help + CCP § 1167; states 10 court days (current AB 2347 rule). >= 1 citation; legal-aid referral present.
inCorpus.demoPoint: Shows the system answers ONLY when it has a cited source, and gives the CURRENT 10-day rule rather than the stale 5-day answer most models give.

outOfCorpus.questionText: How do I get my security deposit back after I move out of my apartment?
outOfCorpus.expectedBehavior: Abstains (retrieval below the confidence gate against the eviction-response corpus) and refers the user to legal aid. citations: []. referral present.
outOfCorpus.demoPoint: Shows the safety feature: rather than guess on a topic outside its indexed California eviction-response sources, it refuses and hands off.
